project_title: Russia Desk - two-page Russian intelligence-and-history analytical desk
runtime_target: source-family to analytic-variable board with uncertainty and judgment gates
operational_end_state: A researcher can route Russia sources into actor interests, institutional constraints, strategic culture, deception patterns, force posture, uncertainty limits, and bounded analytic judgments.
full_build_definition: A runnable local or web application with a defined user flow, input fixture, persisted or exportable output, source-bound mechanism, acceptance tests, and a reviewer-visible claim ceiling.
minimum_operational_release: A runnable slice of Russia Source-To-Judgment Desk: one complete workflow from input to output, with fixture, state record, export, acceptance test, and source-bound claim boundary.
required_modules_to_build: source-family registry | actor interest model | institutional constraint model | strategic culture and deception pattern fields | force posture notes | uncertainty limits | judgment gate
source_status: 7 source-application rows; 0 active passage contracts; 0 unlocated source candidates.
source_actions_before_claim: Carry 7 source-application role(s) into the first build release and convert the most load-bearing source into a close-read contract before any quotation, page-specific citation, or strong literature-positioning claim.
test_suite: claim ceiling test: public claims cannot exceed built evidence | source test: metadata-routed sources require close reading before citation or quotation | privacy test: no local paths or private directories in public deploy
acceptance_tests: Open or run the application slice; feed the fixture; verify output artifact, state persistence or export, error boundary, and source-governed acceptance condition.
promotion_stage: full-build candidate; execute minimum operational release next
current_status: specified proof plan; needs live or replayable demonstrator
launch_or_review_path: Create or open the local runtime slice for `russia-desk`, then execute the fixture-to-output workflow and capture the result.
first_full_build: Run one source family into a visible source-to-variable-to-judgment chain.
next_implementation_steps: 1. create or confirm runtime data schema; 2. implement one end-to-end fixture; 3. capture proof artifact; 4. run source and claim-ceiling gates; 5. publish reviewer-safe demo
public_claim_now: May claim a full-build candidate exists with proof and source contracts; do not claim deployment, outside validation, or production readiness until live execution and passage verification are recorded.
claim_boundary: This is a full-build plan, not a claim that the full operational system has already been completed.
